reasoning: The informal statement's explicit definition of "linear" ($a_0 + a_1 x_1 + \ldots + a_n x_n$) is actually affine (includes constant), while the Lean uses `IsLinearMap ℝ` (strict linear, no constant). However, the two existential claims are logically equivalent: strict linear trivially implies affine, and given any $g$ satisfying the affine property with $(f_i + \partial g/\partial x_i)(x) = b_i + \sum_j a_{ij}x_j$, one can define $g'(x) = g(x) - \sum_i b_i x_i$ so that $f_i + \partial g'/\partial x_i = \sum_j a_{ij}x_j$ is strict linear. This modification works in every model, so the two ∃-statements are equivalent under standard mathematical reading. Other components (ContDiff 2, fderiv with Pi.single for partial derivatives, constants $c_{ij}$, quantifier structure) correctly match the informal statement.
